title: George Washington: Father of Our Nation
description: This coloring page features George Washington, America's first president, in a historic scene. Students will learn about Washington's life and contributions while engaging in a creative activity.
instructions: Color this image of George Washington using colored pencils or crayons. Read the learning facts to discover interesting information about our first president as you color.

content.image: George Washington standing in front of Mount Vernon, his Virginia plantation home. He is wearing his Revolutionary War uniform with a tricorn hat. The scene includes a cherry tree to the side, the American flag with 13 stars, and Washington crossing the Delaware River in the background.

elements.name: George Washington
elements.description: Color his uniform blue with gold buttons and trim. His hair was powdered white.

elements.name: Mount Vernon
elements.description: Color the mansion white with a red roof, surrounded by green gardens.

elements.name: American Flag
elements.description: Color the 13 stars white, the stripes alternating red and white, and the field blue.

elements.name: Cherry Tree
elements.description: Color the trunk brown, leaves green, and cherries red.

elements.name: Delaware River
elements.description: Color the river blue with white caps for the waves.

learningFacts: George Washington was born on February 22, 1732, in Virginia.
He became the first President of the United States in 1789.
Before becoming president, Washington led the Continental Army during the American Revolution.
Washington's home, Mount Vernon, is now a historic site that people can visit.
He served two terms as president and established many traditions that presidents still follow today.
The story about Washington chopping down a cherry tree is a legend and probably didn't actually happen.
Washington's face appears on the U.S. one-dollar bill and the quarter coin.
